text.heading: Senate urges Yar’Adua to intervene in Zimbabwe’s poll crisis
text.p: The Senate yesterday urged President Uma-ru Musa Yar'Adua to join hands with the United Nations (UN), African Union (AU) and other world leaders to encourage President Robert Mugabe to step down.

While urging Yar'Adua to play a leading role to ensure that the crisis does not degenerate into a civil war, the Senate also called on the President to prevail on the Zimbabwean Government and all political actors to respect the wishes of the people.
It made these resolutions during a motion on the Crisis in Zimbabwe moved by Senator Nimi Barigha-Amange.
Barigha-Amange pointed out that Nigeria played a key role in the independence of Southern African countries and should not fold its hands and watch the situation in Zimbabwe degenerate.

While maintaining that over 200,000 Nigerians are residing in Zimbabwe, he said Nigeria as the chairman of the Human Rights organ of the United Nations must support the UN Secretary General's call for postponement of the election due to the level of violence and intimidation in the country.
According to him, the crisis in Zimbabwe is assuming frightening dimension and that Nigeria has to join hands with African countries and the world to call for a free and fair election.
Zimbabwe is billed to hold presidential re-run on Friday.

Senator Victor Ndoma-Egba said: "What is happening in Zimbabwe infringes on human rights. Human rights are an international issue. It would be very sad for us to watch human rights dissipate in Zimbabwe. We have over 200,000 Nigerians in Zimbabwe."

Senator Julius Ucha said: "The motion is timely. It is unfortunate that the election of two months ago is still dragging. The heads of state in Africa have been watching passively as if nothing is happening."
The bill to appropriate N79.4 billion to the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) was stood down yesterday as senators wanted to have gazetted copy of the budget before its consideration.
